skill.name: ai-avatar-video
skill.description: Create AI avatar and talking head videos via inference.sh CLI. Recommended: P-Video-Avatar (fastest, cheapest, built-in TTS). Also: OmniHuman, Fabric, PixVerse. Capabilities: audio-driven avatars, text-to-avatar, lipsync videos, talking head generation, virtual presenters. Use for: AI presenters, explainer videos, virtual influencers, dubbing, marketing videos. Triggers: ai avatar, talking head, lipsync, avatar video, virtual presenter, ai spokesperson, audio driven video, heygen alternative, synthesia alternative, talking avatar, lip sync, video avatar, ai presenter, digital human
skill.summary: Create AI avatar videos from portraits and audio using inference.sh CLI with support for multiple models
skill.author: inference-sh-skills

security_audit.summary: Static scanner flagged 29 external_command patterns and 20 network patterns as potential risks. Upon evaluation, all findings are FALSE POSITIVES: backtick syntax is documentation formatting in markdown code blocks, not actual command execution; hardcoded URLs are legitimate external service documentation links. This is a documentation-only skill describing how to use an external CLI tool. No malicious intent, no user-controlled input, no dangerous patterns. Risk level: LOW.

low_findings.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Syntax in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected 29 instances of backtick syntax (`) in SKILL.md which it interprets as Ruby/shell command execution patterns. These are actually markdown code block delimiters for example commands - they are documentation, not executable code. The skill is a markdown documentation file describing CLI usage, not a Ruby or shell script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations are markdown code blocks containing example belt CLI commands - not actual shell execution. No code in this file is ever executed.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 20 hardcoded URLs in SKILL.md as potential network security concerns. Upon review, these URLs are legitimate external service documentation links (inference.sh, raw.githubusercontent.com, image hosting URLs) which are appropriate for a CLI tool documentation skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs are legitimate external service documentation and example assets for the belt CLI tool. No credential exfiltration or suspicious network behavior detected.

content.user_title: Generate AI Avatar Videos from Portraits
content.value_statement: Creating professional talking head videos requires expensive equipment and editing skills. This skill enables you to generate realistic AI avatar videos from a single portrait image and text or audio input using the inference.sh CLI.
actual_capabilities: Generate talking head videos from portrait images with built-in text-to-speech
Create audio-driven avatar videos synced to external audio files
Produce lipsync videos that match audio to portrait images
Generate multi-character avatar videos with specific character selection
Support multiple avatar models including P-Video-Avatar, OmniHuman, Fabric, and PixVerse
Dub existing videos in different languages using transcription and translation workflows
limitations: Output quality depends on input portrait quality and lighting
Video generation time varies by model from 1.83s/s to 34s/s per video second
Requires internet connection to access inference.sh cloud services
Some models require separate audio generation workflow (no built-in TTS)

use_cases.title: Marketing Teams Create Product Demos
use_cases.description: Generate professional AI presenter videos for product demos and marketing campaigns without expensive video production equipment.

use_cases.title: Educators Build Course Content
use_cases.description: Create explainer videos and course content with virtual presenters. Scale video production without hiring additional presenters.

use_cases.title: Content Creators Localize Videos
use_cases.description: Dub existing videos into multiple languages by transcribing, translating, and generating new synced audio.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Avatar Video with Built-in TTS
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-avatar-video skill to create a talking head video using P-Video-Avatar with the portrait at {portrait_url} and the script: {your_script}
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a quick avatar video with text-to-speech

prompt_templates.title: Avatar from External Audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-avatar-video skill to generate an avatar video synced to the audio file at {audio_url} using the portrait at {portrait_url}
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have pre-recorded audio to sync with an avatar

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Step Portrait Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-avatar-video skill: First generate a portrait image with pruna/p-image using the prompt: {portrait_prompt}. Then create an avatar video using that portrait.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to generate both the portrait and avatar in one workflow

prompt_templates.title: Video Dubbing Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-avatar-video skill to dub a video: First transcribe the video at {video_url}, then translate the transcript, generate speech, and create a lipsync video.
prompt_templates.scenario: When localizing existing videos into different languages

output_examples.input: Create an avatar video for a product walkthrough script about our new feature launch
output_examples.output: Generated talking head video with an AI presenter delivering the script in 1080p resolution. The video shows a professional avatar with realistic lip movements synced to the built-in TTS narration.

output_examples.input: Sync this audio file to a portrait photo for a training video introduction
output_examples.output: Avatar video created with realistic face movements matched to the provided audio. Portrait subject appears to be speaking the audio content with natural expressions and timing.

output_examples.input: Create a series of marketing videos in English, Spanish, and French
output_examples.output: Three avatar videos generated from the same base portrait, each with TTS narration in the specified language. Consistent avatar appearance across all videos.

best_practices: Use high-quality portrait photos with front-facing composition and good lighting for best results
Choose P-Video-Avatar for fastest generation speed and lowest cost with built-in TTS support
Ensure audio files are clear with minimal background noise for accurate lipsync
anti_patterns: Do not use low-resolution or blurry portrait images as they produce poor quality avatar videos
Do not use P-Video-Avatar for multi-character videos - use OmniHuman 1.5 instead
Do not skip the quality check on generated videos - review output before distribution

faq.question: What is the fastest avatar model available?
faq.answer: P-Video-Avatar is the fastest at approximately 1.83 seconds per second of video. It is also the cheapest at $0.025 per second and includes built-in text-to-speech.

faq.question: Which model should I use for multi-character videos?
faq.answer: Use OmniHuman 1.5 for multi-character videos. It supports specifying which character to drive with audio in images containing multiple people.

faq.question: Do all avatar models support text-to-speech?
faq.answer: No. P-Video-Avatar and Fabric 1.0 have built-in TTS. OmniHuman 1.5 and PixVerse Lipsync require you to generate audio separately using a TTS service like Kokoro TTS.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: Most models accept standard image URLs (JPEG, PNG). Portrait images should be front-facing with good lighting for best quality avatar generation.

faq.question: Can I create vertical videos for social media?
faq.answer: Yes. P-Video-Avatar matches the output aspect ratio to the input image. Use 9:16 aspect ratio portraits for vertical social media videos.

faq.question: How do I dub a video into another language?
faq.answer: Use the full workflow: transcribe original video with Fast Whisper, translate the transcript, generate speech in new language with Kokoro TTS, then sync audio to video using LatentSync.
